title: Pleopeltis fallacissima
taxonomy: Pleopeltis fallacissima is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, a group of vascular plants commonly known as polypody ferns. This species belongs to the genus Pleopeltis, which consists of various species of terrestrial and epiphytic ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is a part of the order Polypodiales, which includes over 7,000 species of ferns worldwide. The order Polypodiales is further grouped within the class Polypodiopsida, a class of vascular plants that also includes the order Osmundales and others. The class Polypodiopsida is part of the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns and their allies.

intro: Pleopeltis fallacissima is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, commonly referred to as a type of "air fern" due to its ability to grow on other plants without deriving nutrients from them. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Pleopeltis fallacissima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, with some herbal preparations also being sold as dietary supplements.
